Turn 1 ‚Äî first invariant sweep: kernel abelianization over G I read both uploaded markdown files. The task is to decide whether the two one-relator profinite groups ŒìS,ŒìH are isomorphic over the fixed quotient G=‚ü®œÉ,œÑ‚à£œÑœÉ=œÑ2‚ü©, meaning œÄH‚àòŒ¶=œÄS.The problem statement defines F=P‚ãäG, with P the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1, and defines the two relators rS,rH exactly as quoted.

The first thing worth attacking is the intrinsic G-moduleKiab,Ki:=ker(Œìi‚ÜíG),because any over-G isomorphism ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH necessarily induces a Z2 [[G]]-module isomorphismKSab‚âÖKHab.This remains true even if the isomorphism does not preserve the chosen section G‚ÜíŒìi: the possible section-twist is a 1-cocycle into KH, and its inner conjugation effect vanishes after abelianizing KH.LetŒõ:=Z2 [[G]],and write X0,X1 for the images of x0,x1 in Pab. I use the right-module convention, so xig contributes Xig.A key idempotent appears. In every finite quotient of G, the image of œÑ has odd order: if œÑ has order m, conjugation by œÉ sends œÑ‚Ü¶œÑ2, so t‚Ü¶t2 must be an automorphism of ‚ü®œÑ‚ü©, forcing (2,m)=1. Thus the closureT:=‚ü®œÑ‚ü©is pro-prime-to-2. LetE:=eT‚ààZ2 [[T]]‚äÜŒõ be the Haar averaging idempotent of T. Then, in Pab,[(yœÑ)œÄ2]=E[Y]whenever Y=[y]. In particular,[(x0‚àí3œÑ)œÄ2]=‚àí3EX0,[ui]=[(xiœÑ)œÄ2]=EXi.Linearization of rS

The commutator (x1,x1œÉ2) vanishes in Pab. Hence ‚Ñì(rS)=‚àíX0 œÉ‚àí3EX0+2X1.Equivalently, up to multiplying the relator by ‚àí1, the relation row iswS=(œÉ+3E,‚àí2).SoKSab‚âÖŒõ2/Œõ(œÉ+3E,‚àí2).Linearization of rH

We have[u0]=EX0,[d0]=(E‚àí1)X0,[dg]=(E‚àí1)X0g0,and c0,hc are commutators, hence vanish in the abelianization. Therefore[h0]=X0g0+X0+(E‚àí1)X0g0+(E‚àí1)X0+2(E‚àí1)X0.

This simplifies to[h0]=X0(Eg0+3E‚àí2).Also[u1‚àí1]=‚àíEX1,[x1œÉ]=X1œÉ.Thus ‚Ñì(rH)=X0(Eg0+3E‚àí2)+X1 (œÉ‚àíE),so the relation row iswH=(Eg0+3E‚àí2,œÉ‚àíE).ThusKHab‚âÖŒõ2/Œõ(Eg0+3E‚àí2,œÉ‚àíE).The first obstruction does not distinguish the groups The central idempotent E splits the comparison into E- and (1‚àíE)-parts.

On the (1‚àíE)-part:wS=(œÉ,‚àí2),wH=(‚àí2,œÉ).

These are row-equivalent by the swap matrix(0110).On the E-part, œÑ has been averaged out. Writes:=œÉ2,a:=œÉ 1‚àíœÄ2,so that œÉ=as and g0=s2. The two rows becomewS=(as+3,‚àí2),wH=(s2+1,as‚àí1).Now split the prime-to-2 part generated by a. Let f be the Haar idempotent for the pro-prime-to-2 group‚ü®a‚ü©.

On the f-part, where a=1, the explicit matrixU0=( s‚àí1s‚àí212)has determinant s, a unit, and satisfies (s+3,‚àí2)U0=(s2+1,s‚àí1).On the (1‚àíf)-part, as+3 is a unit: modulo 2, this is as+1, and away from the trivial a-character that element is invertible. Define Œª:=(s2+1)(as+3)‚àí1 on this (1‚àíf)-part. ThenU1=(Œª+2as+312)satisfies (as+3,‚àí2)U1=(s2+1,as‚àí1).

Its determinant is 2Œª+1‚àías,which is again a unit on the (1‚àíf)-part, since modulo 2 it is 1+as, invertible there.

Combining the two pieces,UE:=fU0+(1‚àíf)U1 is an invertible 2√ó2 matrix over EŒõ carrying the E-part of wS to the E-part of wH.So the full abelianized relation rows are equivalent over Œõ. ThereforeKSab‚âÖKHab as Z2 [[G]]-modules.This is a useful negative result: the most obvious over-G obstruction vanishes. In particular, no disproof can come from the kernel‚Äôs first homology, its first Fitting ideal, or any specialization of the abelianized relation module.The matrix above also gives a plausible shape for a future Nielsen/Fox lift: the (1‚àíE)-part wants to swap the two generators, while the E-part performs the 2-adic Euclidean move exchanging the roles of 2 and œÉ‚àí1. The real question is now whether this abelianized change of basis lifts to a continuous automorphism of the free G-operator pro-2 group P carrying the closed normal closure of rS to that of rH, possibly after a cocycle twist of the splitting over G.

ESTABLISHED: rS,rH‚ààP; any over-G isomorphism must identify KSab and KHab as Z2 [[G]]-modules; and this necessary condition passes via an explicit idempotent-by-idempotent row equivalence.

GAP: This does not yet prove ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH, because the row equivalence is only abelianized. It remains to decide whether it lifts through the nonabelian pro-2 Magnus/Fox tower, or whether a degree-‚â•2 obstruction appears.

NEXT: Compute the quadratic Magnus expansions of rS and rH modulo the square of the augmentation/Frattini filtration, transported by the explicit H1 -matrix above; if the quadratic obstruction also vanishes, try to construct an actual G-operator Nielsen automorphism.[BREAKTHROUGH]
